channel.title: Brisbane s calendar Light up Brisbane
channel.description: This calendar includes scheduled lights ups of Council assets including Story Bridge, Reddacliff Place sculptures, Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City Hall, King George Square, William Jolly Bridge and Tropical Dome at Brisbane Botanic Gardens Mt Coot-tha.

item.title: Domestic and Family Violence Prevention Month
item.description:
Sunday, 24 May 2026

Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Purple to support Domestic and Family Violence Prevention Month

Domestic and Family Violence Prevention Month is held each May to raise community awareness of DFV and to send a clear message that violence of this sort will not be tolerated. It is an opportunity to encourage communities and individuals to speak out against violence and abuse and to empower people to work together towards its prevention.

This Light Up is organised by: Department of Families, Seniors, Disability Services and Child Safety

Light up asset: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: Reconciliation Week
item.description:
Monday, 25 May 2026

Reddacliff Place sculptures will be lit all colours to support Reconciliation Week

The theme for National Reconciliation Week 2026, All In, is a powerful invitation for every Australian to step forward with purpose and conviction. It calls on us to embrace reconciliation not as an occasional gesture, but as a daily commitment woven into the way we live, work, and walk together. Reconciliation will not unfold on its own. It will only happen when all of us - every community, every sector, every Australian - commit to being part of the change.

This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane City Council

Light up asset: Reddacliff Place, Brisbane City
Venue address: Reddacliff Place, 266 George Street, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: National Sorry Day
item.description:
Tuesday, 26 May 2026

Reddacliff Place sculptures will be lit all colours to support National Sorry Day

On 26 May 1997 the landmark “Bringing them Home” report was tabled in federal parliament. “Bringing them Home” is the final report of the “National Inquiry into the Separation of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Children from Their Families” and was conducted by the Human Rights and Equal Opportunity Commission (now called the Australian Human Rights Commission) between 1995 and 1997. On 26 May 1998, the first National Sorry Day was held to commemorate the anniversary of the report and remember the grief, suffering and injustice experienced by the stolen generations.

This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane City Council

Light up asset: Reddacliff Place, Brisbane City
Venue address: Reddacliff Place, 266 George Street, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: State of Origin - Game 1 Sydney
item.description:
Wednesday, 27 May 2026

Victoria Bridge will be lit Maroon to celebrate State of Origin - Game 1 Sydney

Experience the all-consuming power of Australia’s greatest sporting rivalry as Origin returns for 2026 with Game I - Accor Stadium, Sydney | Wednesday, 27 May
Get ready to witness an unmatched display of intensity as the game’s best players square off in the ultimate fight for state pride. The only thing that compares to the intensity, ferocity and rivalry on the field, is the all-consuming rivalry that sweeps the fans in the stands.
Ampol State of Origin.
Nothing like it.

This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane Economic Development Agency

Light up asset: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue address: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: Darkness to Daylight
item.description:
Thursday, 28 May 2026

Victoria Bridge will be lit Purple to support Darkness to Daylight

The Darkness to Daylight event is Challenge DV's flagship fundraising and awareness event which consists of a 110km overnight run, 110km monthly running challenge, and a 10km and 3km community mass-participation event. It is a month-long campaign to raise much needed funds to support Domestic Violence prevention programs. www.darknesstodaylight.org

This Light Up is organised by: Challenge DV

Light up asset: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue address: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: LGBTQ+ Domestic Violence Awareness Day
item.description:
Thursday, 28 May 2026

Story Bridge will be lit Rainbow to support LGBTQ+ Domestic Violence Awareness Day

Held annually on 28 May, LGBTQ+ Domestic Violence Awareness Day is the first campaign of its kind in the world.
Its inception in Australia in 2020 was a ground-breaking moment in history when it became the first initiative to spotlight domestic, family and intimate partner violence and abuse occurring in LGBTQ+ communities. LGBTQ+ Domestic Violence Awareness Day has grown to become a global event with hundreds of organisations and government departments from several countries recognising the day including England, France, Wales, the USA, Canada, Brazil, Ireland, New Zealand and the Netherlands.

This Light Up is organised by: LGBTQ Domestic Violence Awareness Foundation

Light up asset: Story Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Story Bridge, Main Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

LGBTQ+ Domestic Violence Awareness Day has grown to become a global event with hundreds of organisations and government departments from several countries recognising the day including England, France, Wales, the USA, Canada, Brazil, Ireland, New Zealand and the Netherlands.
This Light Up is organised by: LGBTQ Domestic Violence Awareness Foundation
item.x-trumba:customfield: Brisbane City Hall, 64 Adelaide Street, Brisbane City

item.title: Fatality Free Friday
item.description:
Friday, 29 May 2026

Story Bridge will be lit Yellow to support Fatality Free Friday

Since its inception in 2007, ARSF’s Fatality Free Friday campaign has continued to expand its operation and is now recognised as one of Australia’s largest national community-based road safety program. ARSF’s Fatality Free Friday initiative is about much more than just a single day - Friday 29 May 2026. It operates on the belief that if we can go one day without a death on the road, we can demonstrate the impact a focus on road safety can have every day of the year.
Road safety is a global issue that ranks as one of the most pressing matters facing society today. The social, economic, and emotional costs are immense, and these figures are poised to increase unless something is done, now. All road users are encouraged to get involved and to take personal responsibility to help make a significant reduction in road trauma. We are also aiming for a longer-term community change.

This Light Up is organised by: Department of Transport and Main Roads

Light up asset: Story Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Story Bridge, Main Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: HMAS Brisbane Freedom of Entry Parade
item.description:
Friday, 29 May 2026

City Hall will be lit Red, White and Blue to celebrate the HMAS Brisbane Freedom of Entry Parade

Over three days, events will be held to showcase Navy's importance to the Australian way of life. The Navy will continue to celebrate its tradition and heritage and we encourage all members of the broader community to come along to the celebrations. The Freedom of Entry Parade is an excellent opportunity for our people who serve, their families and the broader Australian community to come together and recognise the critical role our Navy plays in safeguarding the prosperity of our nation, on and under the sea. 

This Light Up is organised by: The Royal Australian Navy

Light up asset: Brisbane City Hall, Brisbane City
Venue address: Brisbane City Hall, 64 Adelaide Street, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Library, Light up asset

item.title: World MS Day
item.description:
Saturday, 30 May 2026

Reddacliff Place sculptures will be lit Red to support World MS Day

World MS Day is an international awareness day for everyone affected by multiple sclerosis (MS). It brings the global MS community together to share stories, raise awareness and campaign for change. World MS Day is officially marked on the 30 May and is such an important day for people living with MS as they see they are not alone and are supported by the wider community.

This Light Up is organised by: MS Queensland

Light up asset: Reddacliff Place, Brisbane City
Venue address: Reddacliff Place, 266 George Street, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: Red Shield Appeal
item.description:
Sunday, 31 May 2026

Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Red to support Red Shield Appeal

The annual Red Shield Appeal raises awareness of The Salvation Army and financially supports various vital programs that the Salvation Army has been providing communities in Australia, without discrimination, since 1880. We provide active, practical support designed to meet the social, emotional, material, and spiritual needs of those experiencing hardship through poverty, health issues, dependency issues, unemployment and/or experiencing personal crisis

This Light Up is organised by: Salvation Army

Light up asset: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: Reconciliation Week
item.description:
Monday, 1 June 2026

Story Bridge will be lit Black, Red and Yellow with Blue, White and Green to support Reconciliation Week

The theme for National Reconciliation Week 2026, All In, is a powerful invitation for every Australian to step forward with purpose and conviction. It calls on us to embrace reconciliation not as an occasional gesture, but as a daily commitment woven into the way we live, work, and walk together. Reconciliation will not unfold on its own. It will only happen when all of us - every community, every sector, every Australian - commit to being part of the change

This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane City Council

item.title: Italian National Day
item.description:
Tuesday, 2 June 2026

Victoria Bridge will be lit Red, White and Green to celebrate Italian National Day

Celebrate Festa della Repubblica - Italian National Day. This Day commemorates the historic referendum of June 2, 1946, when the Italian people voted to become a republic. By lighting up Brisbane in the colours of the Italian flag, we aim to honour the contributions of the Italian community to the cultural, social, and economic life of Queensland, promote multicultural inclusion and strengthens the connection between Australia and Italy. It is an opportunity for all residents to reflect on shared values of democracy, community, and cultural heritage.

This Light Up is organised by: com.it.es. QLD + NT

Light up asset: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue address: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.xCal:description:
Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Red, White and Green to celebrate Italian National Day
Celebrate Festa della Repubblica - Italian National Day. This Day commemorates the historic referendum of June 2, 1946, when the Italian people voted to become a republic. By lighting up Brisbane in the colours of the Italian flag, we aim to honour the contributions of the Italian community to the cultural, social, and economic life of Queensland, promote multicultural inclusion and strengthens the connection between Australia and Italy. It is an opportunity for all residents to reflect on shared values of democracy, community, and cultural heritage.
This Light Up is organised by: Com.It.Es. QLD + NT

item.title: Mabo Day
item.description:
Wednesday, 3 June 2026

Victoria Bridge will be lit Black, Red and Yellow with Blue, White and Green to support Mabo Day

3 June commemorates Eddie Koiki Mabo, a Torres Strait Islander whose campaign for Indigenous land rights led to a landmark decision of the High Court of Australia that overturned ‘terra nullius’ and led to Australia’s Federal Parliament passing the Native Title Act 1993 which established the legal framework for native title claims throughout Australia by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples

This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane City Council

Light up asset: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue address: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: HS Awareness Week
item.description:
Thursday, 4 June 2026

Victoria Bridge will be lit Purple to support HS Awareness Week

Hidradenitis Suppurativa Australia is dedicated to improving the lives of people living with HS through raising awareness, education and advocacy work.
The objective is to remove barriers to care, provide more supports and get earlier diagnosis. Illuminate4HS is the biggest awareness event in our calendar year that happens globally and celebrated in the first week of June each year. Turning landmarks purple gives people a talking point and way to spark tough conversations about the challenges of living with HS. Every shared story is one more puzzle piece for another on their HS journey

This Light Up is organised by: Hidradenitis Supperativa Australia Ltd

Light up asset: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue address: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: Brisbane Marathon Festival
item.description:
Friday, 5 June 2026

Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Blue to celebrate Brisbane Marathon Festival

Celebrating our 34th year in 2026, the Brisbane Marathon Festival is one of the oldest and most friendly events on the Australian running calendar attracting thousands of participants from over 35 countries annually. The marathon festival includes a half marathon, a 10 km run, a 5 km run/walk and a children's 2 km 'mini marathon'. The event celebrates fun fitness and the test of ultimate human endurance – the Marathon.

This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane Economic Development Agency

Light up asset: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: Queensland Day
item.description:
Saturday, 6 June 2026

Reddacliff Place sculptures will be lit Maroon to celebrate Queensland Day

On 6 June, we mark the anniversary of the day that Queensland stood on its own, officially separating from New South Wales in 1859. It's our day to get together and celebrate our state - our way! This year, we're celebrating everything that makes our state great and we want you to be part of it

This Light Up is organised by: Queensland Government - Department of the Premier and Cabinet

Light up asset: Reddacliff Place, Brisbane City
Venue address: Reddacliff Place, 266 George Street, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: International Myasthenia Gravis Awareness Month
item.description:
Sunday, 7 June 2026

Victoria Bridge will be lit Teal to support International Myasthenia Gravis Awareness Month

June is International Myasthenia Gravis Awareness Month. Myasthenia Gravis (MG) is a chronic autoimmune disorder that disrupts communication between nerves and muscles leading to muscle weakness, fatigue and difficulty with everyday tasks like speaking, swallowing and walking. "Light Up for Myasthenia" is an opportunity to raise awareness in the broader community of this little known rare disease and support those diagnosed with Myasthenia Gravis.

This Light Up is organised by: Myasthenia Gravis Association of Queensland Inc (MGAQ)

Light up asset: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue address: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Teal to support International Myasthenia Gravis Awareness Month
June is International Myasthenia Gravis Awareness Month. Myasthenia Gravis (MG) is a chronic autoimmune disorder that disrupts communication between nerves and muscles leading to muscle weakness, fatigue and difficulty with everyday tasks like speaking, swallowing and walking. "Light Up for Myasthenia" is an opportunity to raise awareness in the broader community of this little known rare disease and support those diagnosed with Myasthenia Gravis.
This Light Up is organised by: Myasthenia Gravis Association of Queensland Inc (MGAQ)
item.x-trumba:customfield: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point

item.title: Big Freeze 12
item.description:
Monday, 8 June 2026

Story Bridge will be lit Blue to support Big Freeze 12

The Big Freeze is FightMND’s largest fundraising campaign, raising awareness and vital funds for MND research and projects supporting those living with MND. Whilst Monday 8 June 2026,is our largest event at the MCG as brave celebrities take the plunge into Australia’s coldest ice bath ahead of the Collingwood vs Melbourne King’s Birthday clash, we're not stopping there! We’re freezing the nation with State Freeze events! A football game is all about competition between two opposing sides. When it comes to the fight against the Beast, the Big Freeze shows that we’re all on the same team, united in our goal of a world without MND

This Light Up is organised by: Fight MND

Light up asset: Story Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Story Bridge, Main Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: Thank a First Responder Day
item.description:
Tuesday, 9 June 2026

Reddacliff Place sculptures will be lit Teal to support Thank a First Responder Day

Thank a First Responder Day, a Fortem Australia initiative, is a national day of appreciation for first responders and their families. Held annually on the second Wednesday in June, it is a day for communities across Australia to show their gratitude to local first responders by simply saying “Thank you”

This Light Up is organised by: Fortem Australia Ltd

item.title: Brisbane Festival Program Announcement
item.description:
Wednesday, 10 June 2026

Story Bridge will be lit Pink to celebrate Brisbane Festival Program Announcement

The celebration of the nation’s favourite springtime festival and Australia’s largest international arts festival begins with the first glimpse of the program – released today June 10. Brisbane is alive with creativity, celebration and community connection, and the Brisbane Festival is a festival like no other, born from a city unlike any other.

This Light Up is organised by: Major Brisbane Festivals Ltd

item.title: Lipoedema Awareness Month
item.description:
Thursday, 11 June 2026

Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Purple to support Lipoedema Awareness Month

June is Lipoedema Awareness Month, with an estimated 11% to 19% of the female population have it. It is frequently misdiagnosed due to the lack of awareness of the condition, meaning that many women are unaware they have it. Lipoedema Australia is the national representative body for lipoedema. As a registered charity, we are committed to directing and supporting research and medical recognition of lipoedema, developing a comprehensive treatment/management plan and ultimately finding a cure. We are also dedicated to improving the lives of individuals affected by lipoedema.

This Light Up is organised by: Lipoedema Autralia Ltd

Light up asset: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: Philippine Independence Day
item.description:
Friday, 12 June 2026

Victoria Bridge will be lit Red, White Blue to support Philippine Independence Day

The Philippine Declaration of Independence was proclaimed on June 12th 1898. On 12 June 2026, the Chamber of Commerce and Industry Australia Philippines Incorporated would like to reach out Brisbane and its Filipino-Australians, and all residents to join together to celebrate the Philippines 128th Independence Day.

This Light Up is organised by: The Chamber of Commerce and Industry Australia Philippines Incorporated

item.title: Brissie to the Bay
item.description:
Saturday, 13 June 2026

Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Red to support Brissie to the Bay

Join the Ride of Champions from Brisbane's International Cycle Park to the pristine Bay and back on Sunday 14 June, 2026! A distance and challenge for all abilities, with every km you ride and every dollar you raise, making a life-changing impact. Be a champion and help us raise $1,000,000 to fund vital support services. You can ensure no one living with a neurological condition faces their journey alone.

This Light Up is organised by: Multtiple Sclerosis Queensland Ltd

Light up asset: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: Eve of World Elder Abuse Awareness Day
item.description:
Sunday, 14 June 2026

Victoria Bridge will be lit Purple to support Eve of World Elder Abuse Awareness Day

World Elder Abuse Awareness Day (WEAAD) is a day to voice opposition to the abuse of older people. It is an annual initiative launched by the International Network for the Prevention of Elder Abuse and the World Health Organisation. WEAAD is commemorated each year on June 15 to highlight one of the worst manifestations of ageism and inequality in our society - elder abuse. What is elder abuse? Elder abuse is a single or repeated act - or lack of appropriate action - occurring within any relationship where there is an expectation of trust, which causes harm or distress to an older person. The six abuse sub-types are commonly recognised as financial abuse, physical abuse, sexual abuse, psychological abuse (otherwise known as emotional abuse), social abuse, and neglect.

This Light Up is organised by: UnitingCare Queensland Limited

Light up asset: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue address: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: Go Green For Ecomarines
item.description:
Tuesday, 16 June 2026

Victoria Bridge will be lit Green to support Go Green For Ecomarines

Go Green for EcoMarines is a month-long campaign to raise awareness and funds to support an education-based action program targeting a reduction in waste and pollution and promoting recycling and other sustainable living options. EcoMarines Foundation started in 2014 with just six schools, and we are now in over 300 schools and Early Learning Centres across Queensland

This Light Up is organised by: Ecomarines Foundation

Light up asset: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue address: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: State of Origin - Game 2 Melbourne
item.description:
Wednesday, 17 June 2026

Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Maroon to celebrate State of Origin - Game 2 Melbourne

Experience the all-consuming power of Australia’s greatest sporting rivalry as Origin returns for 2026 with Game II at the mighty MCG, Melbourne | Wednesday, 17 June
Get ready to witness an unmatched display of intensity as the game’s best players square off in the ultimate fight for state pride. The only thing that compares to the intensity, ferocity and rivalry on the field, is the all-consuming rivalry that sweeps the fans in the stands.
Ampol State of Origin.
Nothing like it.

This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane Economic Development Agency

Light up asset: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: State of Origin - Game 2 Melbourne
item.description:
Wednesday, 17 June 2026

Story Bridge will be lit Maroon to celebrate State of Origin - Game 2 Melbourne

Experience the all-consuming power of Australia’s greatest sporting rivalry as Origin returns for 2026 with Game II at the mighty MCG, Melbourne | Wednesday, 17 June
Get ready to witness an unmatched display of intensity as the game’s best players square off in the ultimate fight for state pride. The only thing that compares to the intensity, ferocity and rivalry on the field, is the all-consuming rivalry that sweeps the fans in the stands.
Ampol State of Origin.
Nothing like it.

This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane Economic Development Agency

Light up asset: Story Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Story Bridge, Main Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: World Sickle Cell Day
item.description:
Friday, 19 June 2026

Reddacliff Place sculptures will be lit Red to support World Sickle Cell Day

World Sickle Cell Day, which takes place on June 19 each year, is an internationally recognised awareness day promoting equitable access to treatment of those living with sickle cell anaemia and to break down stigma in the community. Lighting landmarks in red help’s raise public awareness of Sickle Cell, sparks conversation, and increases understanding and acknowledge the experiences of individuals and families affected.

This Light Up is organised by: Thalassaemia and Sickle Cell Australia

Light up asset: Reddacliff Place, Brisbane City
Venue address: Reddacliff Place, 266 George Street, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: Migraine Awareness Month/Shades for Migraine Day
item.description:
Sunday, 21 June 2026

Victoria Bridge will be lit Purple to support Migraine Awareness Month/Shades for Migraine Day

Shades for Migraine - Sunday, June 21: Wear sunglasses to symbolize sensitivity and post a selfie with the hashtag #ShadesForMigraine.
Migraine Australia is the national patient advocacy organisation for people living with migraine, their carers and families. We strive to be the leading source of information about migraine, collaborating with all stakeholders on behalf of people impacted by migraine. Led by people who have lived experience with migraine or who care for those who do. They are encouraged to join the our online support groups, website and social media spaces to keep informed.

This Light Up is organised by: Migraine Australia Ltd

Light up asset: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue address: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

item.title: The 65th Annual Queen's Ball
item.description:
Sunday, 21 June 2026

City Hall will be lit Rainbow to celebrate The 65th Annual Queen's Ball

The Queens Ball is a flagship annual event that honours outstanding contributions to the LGBTIQA+ community while raising critical funds to support ongoing programs, grants, and advocacy work. It brings together community members, leaders, and allies in a formal setting that fosters connection, visibility, and shared identity, while also reinforcing the community's values and recognising its history and progress

This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane Pride

Light up asset: Brisbane City Hall, Brisbane City
Venue address: Brisbane City Hall, 64 Adelaide Street, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Library, Light up asset
